metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add concrete jq assertion patterns for smoke-check checks (2) and (3), e.g.: check (2) `curl ... | jq -e '[.[].username] | index("RebarBotNava") != null'`; check (3) `curl ... | jq -e '.local["refs/heads/*"].permissions.rebase.rules | has("Contributors")'`.
findings.finding: Smoke-check assertions for checks (2) and (3) are described only in prose ('RebarBotNava is a member', 'shows the rebase permission granted to Contributors') without specifying a concrete jq assertion or grep anchor. The new smoke-check-autolander.sh file could be implemented with a plain substring grep, risking a false-positive (e.g., a different group named 'SubContributors' matching 'Contributors').
findings.location: AC Tests (AC3) — smoke-check-autolander.sh checks (2) and (3)
findings.suggested_fix: In AC5, append to check (2): 'assert with `jq -e \'[.[].username] | index("RebarBotNava") != null\''`'; and to check (3): 'assert with `jq -e \'(.local["refs/heads/*"].permissions.rebase.rules // {}) | has("Contributors")\''`', anchored to the exact ref and group name.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the out-of-repo `access-control.html` citation with a verifiable in-repo evidence anchor for the `rebase` ACL token: add a comment in `project.config` citing the specific Gerrit 3.14.x admin docs URL or a grep of the live instance's `access-control.html` (e.g., via `curl https://<host>/Documentation/access-control.html | grep -A3 'rebase'`) captured in a runbook or the script itself as a preflight assertion.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `rebase` is the correct Gerrit 3.14.1 project.config [access] token for gating the rebase-on-behalf REST path, citing 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html' — a file that is not present in the repository and cannot be probed. The smoke-check step 3 (GET /a/projects/rebar/access) only reads back the token the plan pushed, so it cannot distinguish a correctly-named token from a silently-accepted-but-inert one. If the token is wrong, provisioning passes but the autolander 403s at runtime.
findings.location: AC: Rebase-on-behalf access right (concrete) — 'project.config [access] token rebase, confirmed in this instance's access-control.html'
findings.suggested_fix: In smoke-check-autolander.sh, add a preflight step that fetches the live instance's access-control docs page and greps for the `rebase` token to confirm the token name matches before the ACL push. Alternatively, capture the token name from a live `ssh admin@host gerrit ls-groups` + `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` call on the existing instance and record it as a commented, in-repo cross-check in project.config.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the Fallback Protocol AC: either (a) explicitly state that the 403 detection applies only when the call is made in S2a/E2E (not in this task's smoke check), or (b) add a live rebase-on-behalf call as a fourth smoke-check step and reconcile with the read-only constraint.
findings.finding: The Fallback Protocol AC names 'the AC3 smoke test' as the checkpoint that detects a '403 on the live rebase-on-behalf call,' but Tests (AC3) explicitly defines the smoke check as read-only with three ACL/auth verification checks — it does NOT make a live rebase-on-behalf REST call. The fallback's claimed detection mechanism is absent from AC3's actual scope, sending the implementer in two directions: is the smoke check supposed to also exercise a live rebase call, or does the fallback protocol describe out-of-scope behavior?
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Fallback protocol' AC vs 'Tests (AC3)' AC
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the Fallback Protocol AC to read: 'If the live rebase-on-behalf call 403s when exercised in S2a or the epic E2E (outside this task's read-only smoke check), the 403 body names the missing right; the remediation is to add that right to the same refs/heads/* stanza. This task's AC3 smoke check (read-only, check 3) confirms the grant is present before S2a runs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC3 and AC4: either (a) add a live (but isolated/reversible) rebase-on-behalf call to the smoke check so AC4's 403-detection trigger is actually exercised, or (b) rewrite AC4 to state that the fallback activates when AC3 check (3) fails (`rebase` permission absent in the /access response) — not on a live rebase 403.
findings.finding: AC4 (Fallback Protocol) names 'the AC3 smoke test' as the checkpoint that detects 'the live rebase-on-behalf call 403s,' but AC3 (Tests) explicitly defines the smoke check as read-only with zero live rebase calls. The decision rule (AC4) references a trigger event its co-located test (AC3) structurally cannot produce, leaving the fallback protocol with no defined activation path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Fallback protocol (no silent failure)' (AC4) and 'Tests (AC3)'
findings.suggested_fix: In AC4, replace 'if the live rebase-on-behalf call 403s despite the rebase grant' with 'if smoke-check step (3) shows the `rebase` permission absent from the /a/projects/rebar/access response' — this aligns the fallback trigger with the actual read-only check AC3 defines.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the plan title to match the body's actual scope, e.g. 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase-on-behalf ACL provisioning' — removing the 'All-Projects capability' and 'submit ACL' phrases that the body explicitly repudiates.
findings.finding: The plan title advertises 'All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning' but the plan body explicitly does the opposite: the rebase grant is placed on the rebar project (NOT All-Projects), and the body explicitly states 'Submit is already covered — do NOT add a submit grant.' A developer tasked via the title alone would build the wrong thing.
findings.location: Plan title: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning' vs. AC lines 'Submit is already covered — do NOT add a submit grant' and 'NOT `All-Projects`'
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase-on-behalf ACL provisioning for autolander bot' — accurately reflecting that the work is (a) an SSM SecureString for the autolander token and (b) a `rebase` grant on the rebar project's `refs/heads/*`, with no All-Projects change and no new submit grant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename the plan title to reflect what IS done (e.g., 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase ACL on rebar project + autolander smoke check') and remove the 'All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning' framing from the title.
findings.finding: The plan title contains a pink-elephant antipattern: 'All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning' names exactly the two things the plan body explicitly instructs NOT to do (grant a global All-Projects capability, add a new submit ACL). An implementer reading only the title or a task-list summary gets an inverted mental model of the work.
findings.location: Plan title: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning'
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + rebar-project Rebase ACL + autolander smoke check provisioning'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename the title to remove the Gerrit-ambiguous term 'All-Projects capability' (e.g. 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase ACL + submit ACL provisioning') and add a one-line note in the Files section clarifying this is a per-project access right, not a global capability.
findings.finding: The title says 'All-Projects capability' — a Gerrit-specific term meaning a global capability defined in All-Projects — but the body explicitly instructs NOT to touch All-Projects. An executor reading the title first may begin editing All-Projects before the body corrects them.
findings.location: Plan title / Files section (infra/gerrit/project.config)
findings.suggested_fix: Change title to: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + per-project Rebase ACL + Submit ACL provisioning'. In the Files section change 'grant the bot the Rebase access right' → 'grant the Contributors group the Rebase access right on refs/heads/* in the rebar project (NOT a global All-Projects capability)' to match AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note in the Files section (or in the ssm.tf edit description) explicitly stating that `infra/terraform/user_data.sh` does NOT need updating — the autolander token is a runtime credential consumed only by `fetch-secrets.sh` → `infra/compose/.env`, not by cloud-init's `/etc/rebar/.env` bootstrap.
findings.finding: user_data.sh is an explicitly named consumer of `local.rebar_secret_params` (the ssm.tf comment at line 16 reads 'keep in sync with user_data.sh and ADR 0012'), yet the plan lists it as neither an edit target nor an explicitly excluded file. user_data.sh maintains its own independent PARAMS map (lines 88–102) for SSM→/etc/rebar/.env; however the autolander token is a runtime credential (not a boot-time secret), so user_data.sh intentionally does NOT need updating. The plan's silence on this, combined with ssm.tf's explicit sync directive, will confuse the implementer.
findings.location: Files section / infra/terraform/ssm.tf line 16 comment
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` edit bullet: '`user_data.sh` does NOT need updating — that file's PARAMS map covers only boot-time secrets needed for /etc/rebar/.env; the autolander token is consumed at runtime via fetch-secrets.sh.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the deployment sequencing gate for the `autolander-gerrit-token` SSM slot: either (a) document 'populate the SSM slot before merging/deploying this change' as a prerequisite step, OR (b) justify using `get_param_optional` (with a downstream fail-loud guard in the container entrypoint when the autolander is active) to decouple this credential from the general instance boot path — matching the pattern established for the OAuth creds.
findings.finding: The plan mandates `get_param` (required/fail-fast) for `autolander-gerrit-token` in `fetch-secrets.sh`, but does not specify the deployment sequencing needed to prevent boot failures on existing instances. Once this change merges and `fetch-secrets.sh` is updated, any reboot or re-run of `fetch-secrets.sh` before an operator populates the SSM slot beyond the CHANGEME sentinel will abort boot — silently breaking an otherwise healthy instance. The plan provides no operator gate (e.g., 'populate the SSM slot before merging/deploying' or 'this is acceptable because the autolander is not yet started') to close this timing window.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Credential bullet: 'fetched by fetch-secrets.sh using get_param (required/fail-fast — NOT get_param_optional) so a missing token aborts boot'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prerequisite step to the plan: 'Before merging this change, an operator MUST populate `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` in SSM (via `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite`). Terraform creates the placeholder; the real token must be set before any instance reboot.' Alternatively, if the autolander container won't be started until a later story, consider using `get_param_optional` here and adding a fail-loud guard in the autolander container's own entrypoint — matching the OAuth creds pattern and the rationale documented in fetch-secrets.sh lines 15-19.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Files section description to say 'grant the **Contributors group** the Rebase access right on refs/heads/*' so it matches the concrete AC3 stanza (`rebase = group Contributors`) and removes the 'grant the bot' phrasing.
findings.finding: The Files section says 'grant the bot the Rebase access right' (implying the grant is to RebarBotNava directly), but AC3 specifies the grant is to the Contributors *group* on refs/heads/*. An executor following the Files section description could produce a user-level grant instead of a group grant.
findings.location: Files section — infra/gerrit/project.config + infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Files bullet to: 'EDIT: add `[access "refs/heads/*"] rebase = group Contributors` to the rebar project's refs/meta/config via setup-project.sh/project.config (NOT All-Projects, NOT a user-level grant).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the exact Gerrit 3.14.1 `project.config` token name for the rebase-on-behalf permission by checking the live instance's `/a/projects/rebar/access` response or the Gerrit 3.14.x source/docs (not just `access-control.html`), and add the verified token name (or a link to the Gerrit 3.14.x changelog/access-control doc) to the plan.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the correct Gerrit `project.config` access-right token for the rebase-on-behalf grant is `rebase` (stanza: `[access "refs/heads/*"] rebase = group Contributors`), citing it is 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html'. This HTML file is not present in the repository and cannot be probed; there is no existing `rebase` stanza in the checked-in `project.config` to cross-reference. The token name is load-bearing: an incorrect token would be silently accepted by the `refs/meta/config` push but have no effect, causing AC3's smoke-check (3) to succeed while the actual REST rebase call still 403s.
findings.location: AC: Rebase-on-behalf access right (concrete) — `[access "refs/heads/*"] rebase = group Contributors`; plan cites 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Gerrit service-account provisioning step (script or explicit operator runbook) for the autolander — analogous to `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` — that creates the autolander Gerrit account, generates its HTTP token, and populates `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` in SSM; without this the SSM slot stays `CHANGEME` and `get_param` aborts boot.
findings.finding: The plan provisions the SSM slot (with CHANGEME sentinel) and wires fetch-secrets.sh to read it, but never specifies how the autolander's Gerrit service account is created and how its HTTP token gets populated into SSM. The review-bot has service-user.sh for this (creates the Gerrit account, generates a token, writes it to SSM). Without an equivalent step for the autolander, the slot stays CHANGEME at boot time, get_param (fail-fast) aborts, and the service never starts.
findings.location: Files section / AC: Credential bullet; fetch-secrets.sh EDIT
findings.suggested_fix: Add `infra/gerrit/setup-autolander-service-user.sh` (mirroring service-user.sh) to the Files list: it creates a `rebar-autolander` Gerrit service account in the `Service Users` group, generates an HTTP token, and runs `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite --name /rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token --value $TOKEN`. Reference this script in AC1 as the operator step that moves the slot from CHANGEME to a real token.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `smoke-check-autolander.sh` uses `curl -f` (or explicit HTTP-status variable checks) and `--max-time`/`--connect-timeout` flags so any non-2xx response or network error causes a non-zero exit, not a silent pass.
findings.finding: The smoke-check script specifies handling for 200 and 403 HTTP responses but does not define behavior for unexpected status codes (5xx, network timeouts, curl connection errors). A Gerrit outage, a reverse-proxy returning 502, or a misconfigured URL would cause curl to exit with a non-200/non-403 code that the script may silently treat as a pass (if not using `set -e` or explicit status-code checks), producing false confidence before production deployment.
findings.location: AC: Tests (AC3) — `infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh`; Fallback protocol AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the smoke-check spec: each curl invocation uses `curl -sf --max-time 10` and the script opens with `set -euo pipefail`; any unexpected status exits 1 with the raw HTTP status code and response body printed to stderr.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a comment in `infra/gerrit/project.config` (or a note in CLAUDE.md / an ADR) explaining that `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` causes Gerrit to ignore All-Projects submit/rebase inheritance for `refs/heads/*`, so all relevant access rights must be granted in this file.
findings.finding: The architectural decision that the Rebase grant must live in the rebar project's own `refs/meta/config` (not `All-Projects`) because `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` would eclipse an All-Projects submit grant is captured only in this plan's prose. Future maintainers editing `project.config` or `All-Projects` without access to this plan will not see the constraint and may introduce a redundant or conflicting grant, or incorrectly remove the project-level grant believing it is covered by inheritance.
findings.location: AC: Rebase-on-behalf access right (concrete); Files: `infra/gerrit/project.config`
findings.suggested_fix: In project.config, directly above the `[access "refs/heads/*"]` stanza, add: `# exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit is set below; All-Projects submit/rebase inheritance is eclipsed for refs/heads/* — all grants must be explicit here.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare HTTPS explicitly for all three `curl` calls in `smoke-check-autolander.sh` (e.g., specify `https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/…` as the URL pattern in AC5) so the transport scheme is unambiguous on the boundary-crossing path that carries `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`.
findings.finding: The smoke-check `curl` calls in AC5 use abbreviated URLs (`...`) that do not explicitly mandate HTTPS, leaving the transport scheme undeclared on a path that transmits the `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` credential; if implemented as HTTP, the token traverses the network base64-encoded (plaintext-equivalent).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (Tests / smoke-check-autolander.sh), check (1): `curl -s -u "$CRED" .../a/accounts/self`
findings.suggested_fix: In AC5, replace the abbreviated `...` URL pattern with an explicit `https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/…` form, e.g.: `curl -s -u "$CRED" "https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/accounts/self"` — mirroring how fetch-secrets.sh already enforces fail-fast on the SSM side; optionally add `--fail` so a non-200 response aborts the smoke check.

findings.finding: The smoke-check step 3 references 'a distinct throwaway identity' to push the synthetic change, but nowhere in the plan is that identity named, its credential sourced, or instructions given for creating it. An executor cannot implement step 3 — or make step 4's uploader-preservation assertion non-vacuous — without knowing what this identity is.
findings.location: AC5 (Tests), smoke-check-autolander.sh step 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a named test account (e.g. `rebar-test-uploader`) with its token at `/rebar/prod/test-uploader-gerrit-token` (same SSM prefix; fetched as `TEST_UPLOADER_GERRIT_TOKEN`). Step 3 pushes using `TEST_UPLOADER_GERRIT_TOKEN`; step 4 asserts the returned `uploader.username` equals `rebar-test-uploader`, not `RebarBotNava`.

findings.finding: The smoke-check AC (step 3) requires pushing a synthetic change as 'a distinct throwaway identity' so 'uploader preserved is observable', but the plan nowhere names that identity, specifies its credential source, or describes how it is provisioned — making smoke-check-autolander.sh unimplementable as written for step 3 and the uploader-preservation assertion in step 4.
findings.location: AC Tests (AC3), step 3: 'push a synthetic change to refs/for/scratch-autolander (uploaded as a distinct throwaway identity so "uploader preserved" is observable)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'The throwaway uploader is the existing `gerrit-admin` account (or a dedicated `smoke-uploader` service account); its HTTP token is passed as `$UPLOADER_CRED` — an env var the operator sets before running the script (or read from `/rebar/prod/gerrit-admin-password` via SSM for the admin account). The script verifies `uploader.username == <that account name>`.' This keeps the script self-contained and the assertion meaningful.

findings.finding: The AC3 smoke test step 5 calls for submitting the synthetic change into scratch-autolander, but the rebar project's active submit-requirements (LLM-Review=MAX AND Verified=MAX AND -has:unresolved) apply to all refs/heads/* — including the scratch-autolander branch. Without explicit bot votes casting LLM-Review=+1 and Verified=+1 on the synthetic change beforehand, the submit call will be rejected with a 409 (submit requirement not satisfied), not a 403. The plan does not specify how to satisfy these gate labels for the throwaway change.
findings.location: AC — Tests (AC3), step 5; infra/gerrit/project.config [submit-requirement 'LLM-Review'] and [submit-requirement 'Verified']
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) before the step-5 submit call, use the admin credential to POST to `/a/changes/{id}/revisions/current/review` with `{"labels":{"LLM-Review":1,"Verified":1}}` to satisfy both requirements; or (b) add a per-branch submit-requirement override in project.config scoped to `refs/heads/scratch-autolander` with `applicableIf = is:false` so the smoke branch bypasses the gate entirely — then document that override clearly as test infrastructure.

findings.finding: The smoke-check step 3 protocol references 'a distinct throwaway identity' as the push actor, but this identity (Gerrit username, email, HTTP token or SSH key, and how/where it is provisioned) is never defined anywhere in the plan. An executor cannot implement the uploader-preservation assertion without knowing what identity to use.
findings.location: AC Tests (AC3), step 3: 'push a synthetic change to refs/for/scratch-autolander (uploaded as a distinct throwaway identity so "uploader preserved" is observable)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dedicated Gerrit account (e.g., `smoke-uploader`) with an HTTP password stored in SSM at `/rebar/prod/smoke-uploader-token`; specify `git config user.name 'Smoke Uploader'` and `user.email smoke-uploader@navateam.com` in the clone used for the push. The AC assertion then checks `.uploader.username == 'smoke-uploader'`.

findings.finding: The smoke test step 3 requires pushing a synthetic change 'as a distinct throwaway identity so uploader preserved is observable,' but no second Gerrit account for that throwaway identity is provisioned or referenced anywhere in this plan or its dependencies. Without a pre-existing second account, the bot and the uploader are the same identity, making the 'uploader ≠ RebarBotNava' assertion in step 4 impossible to satisfy, and the test would pass vacuously or fail with a push-auth error.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests (AC3), step 3

findings.finding: The AC3 smoke test step 3 pushes a synthetic commit to refs/for/scratch-autolander, but the rebar project.config enforces `requireSignedOffBy = true` globally on all refs/for/* pushes. A synthetic commit without a Signed-off-by trailer will be rejected by Gerrit at the receive-pack stage, causing the smoke test to fail at step 3 before it can test the rebase right at all.
findings.location: AC — Tests (AC3), step 3; infra/gerrit/project.config [receive] requireSignedOffBy = true
findings.suggested_fix: In smoke-check-autolander.sh step 3, when constructing the synthetic commit, append `--trailer 'Signed-off-by: Autolander Smoke <smoke@navateam.com>'` to the `git commit` invocation, matching the pattern in feature-branch-e2e.sh line 272.

findings.finding: The smoke-check step 3 pushes a synthetic commit to `refs/for/scratch-autolander`, but the live `project.config` enforces `receive.requireSignedOffBy = true` on all `refs/for/*` pushes. The plan specifies no Signed-off-by trailer handling for the synthetic commit, so the push will be rejected by Gerrit before the rebase step (step 4) is reached.
findings.location: AC Tests (AC3), step 3; infra/gerrit/project.config line 40 ([receive] requireSignedOffBy = true)
findings.suggested_fix: In the smoke-check-autolander.sh spec, after cloning the repo for the step 3 push, add: install the Gerrit commit-msg hook (`curl .../tools/hooks/commit-msg`), set `git config user.email` and `user.name`, and use `git commit -s -m 'smoke: autolander test'` (the `-s` flag appends Signed-off-by automatically). This mirrors the pattern in feature-branch-e2e.sh.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
